_comment: Coverage gate configuration (FEAT-00.2 / STORY-00.2.3, #106). SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
for the enforced line-coverage threshold, the expected-assembly provenance allowlist, and
the ratcheting policy. Read by tools/coverage/coverage-gate.py, which the CI 'coverage' job
runs after collecting coverage (a separate job from 'build-test-format'; see quality-gates.md).
Prose policy lives in docs/engineering/design/quality-gates.md; the numbers here are what
expectedAssembliesNotes: FAIL-CLOSED provenance allowlist. Every production assembly listed here MUST appear in the
merged Cobertura report set with measurable lines; if any is absent (a lost/crashed/dropped
test suite, or a report deleted to game the number) the gate EXITS NON-ZERO naming the missing
assembly, rather than letting it silently drop out of the denominator and inflate coverage.
Add a new src/ production assembly here when it lands so its coverage cannot go unmeasured.
ratchetNotes: The threshold only ever goes UP. When a PR raises measured coverage with real headroom
over the current floor, bump minimumLineCoverage toward (but not above) the measured
value in the same or a follow-up PR so the gain cannot silently erode later. Never lower
this number to make a red build pass; fix the tests instead. The gate prints a ratchet
Floor 87.0 set for FEAT-00.2 against a measured merged 89.16% (see quality-gates.md): a ~2-point
buffer below the locally-measured value absorbs cross-platform variance (local arm64/macOS vs CI
x64/Linux) and run-to-run nondeterminism, so the gate flags genuine regressions rather than noise
AND actively guards the 85->89 range that a wider buffer left unenforced. Ratchet upward toward

ratchetOverride.policy: A floor DECREASE is permitted ONLY when it is tied to CODE REMOVAL that deleted
ratchetOverride.policyCont: well-tested code (which legitimately lowers the measured percentage). Any decrease MUST
ratchetOverride.policyCont2: record its justification here (the removed component + the PR/issue) so a genuine dip is
ratchetOverride.policyCont3: not mistaken for a regression, and so the decrease is auditable. Absent code removal, the
ratchetOverride.policyCont4: floor is monotonic non-decreasing — add or fix tests instead of lowering it.

measurement.mergeSemantics: A production line is covered if hits > 0 in ANY report; totals are the union of distinct (file, line) pairs across all reports, so multi-TFM runs of the same assembly are de-duplicated rather than double-counted.
measurement.excludedAssemblies: [*.Tests] test projects and [DeltaSharp.Samples.*] example apps, plus any assembly/member marked [ExcludeFromCodeCoverage] / [GeneratedCode] / [CompilerGenerated] (see coverlet.runsettings)
measurement.testSupportCaveat: The [*.Tests] exclusion keys on the '.Tests' assembly-name suffix. A test-SUPPORT assembly that does NOT end in '.Tests' (e.g. a shared 'DeltaSharp.TestFixtures' helper) would match [DeltaSharp.*] and leak into the denominator. If such an assembly is added, exclude it explicitly in coverlet.runsettings (e.g. [DeltaSharp.TestFixtures]*) or name it with a '.Tests'-matching suffix.
